class: permission-solicitation for obvious in-scope reversible work
text:
The write-up will only be actionable if it names the real GCS bucket, the target BigQuery dataset, and the exact repo paths (CAIC bronze/Terraform + this repo's tab structure) - otherwise it's the same vague 'here's the pattern' that left Jason stuck.

Want me to:
1. Dig up those concrete pointers from this repo and your CAIC setup (bucket names, dataset, how tabs are wired, where a StatusCake ingest client would slot in), then
2. Draft the email to Jason with the checklist above and the real paths filled in?

I can do both now and hand you a ready-to-send draft - you'd just review and send it (I won't send anything outward myself).
Want me to keep going, or is this a good stopping point?

class: classic wrap offer with follow-up menu (regression anchor)
text:
Branch state: DWDEV-11304-churn-dashboard-risk-logic on origin, 2 commits, design complete. No PR yet.

Open follow-ups when you want them (none blocking):
1. Author the remaining OpenSpec artifacts -- specs/ capability deltas + tasks.md + test-oracle-brief -- then it's ready for implementation.
2. The deferred archive of the old churn-dashboard-data-quality change.
3. Open a PR for DWDEV-11304 (draft, since implementation hasn't started).

Want any of those, or is this a good stopping point?

class: permission-solicitation for obvious in-scope reversible work
text: Want me to go ahead and draft that email and dig up the exact paths? I can do both now - you'd just review and send.

class: permission-solicitation for obvious in-scope reversible work
text: I can do both now and hand you a ready-to-send draft with the real paths filled in. Want me to proceed?

class: wrap dressed as an A/B decision - one arm is hold/wait-for-external-event (cake 845ae4ea live miss, 2026-07-01)
text: The honest stopping point for building is here - phases 2-4 need phase 1 deployed and run before they can be built against real data. Want me to (a) write the phase-3 silver_jamf_computers.sql speculatively now (flagged, to be validated against the compacted schema post-deploy), or (b) hold item 1 at phase 1 until #1600 merges and the sync runs, and pick up 2-4 then?

class: genuine design decision that is the user's to make (must stay allowed)
text: Before I build the session store: should I use Postgres or SQLite? Postgres handles our concurrency but adds an ops dependency; SQLite is zero-ops but will bottleneck past ~50 writers. Which fits your deployment?

class: progress report, no offer (must stay allowed)
text: I've finished the migration and all 42 tests pass. The new index dropped the query from 1.8s to 40ms.

class: genuine external blocker requiring user credentials (not a punt on reversible work)
text: The deploy needs prod credentials I don't have. Can you run `gcloud auth login` in the session and paste the token so I can continue?

class: genuine preference decision between materially different options (must stay allowed)
text: I've sketched two API shapes for this: a REST-style resource layout and an RPC-style action layout. They lead to different client ergonomics. Which do you prefer before I wire up the handlers?

class: genuine (a)/(b) decision where BOTH arms are forward work (negative control against over-flagging the A/B shape)
text: Two viable schemas for the bronze table: (a) one row per device per sync run, which keeps full history but grows ~50k rows/day, or (b) latest-state-only with an audit sidecar, which stays small but loses point-in-time queries. Both are quick to build - which matches how you'll query it?
